sections.paragraph:
θαυμαστός, ή, όν (s. next entry; Hom. Hymns +) pert. to being a cause of wonder or worthy of amazement, wonderful, marvelous, remarkable (Diod S 1, 36, 7 θ. is heightened to παντελῶς ἄπιστον), in our lit. not of human personalities, but of God (Da 9:4 Theod.; Ps 92:4b) 1 Cl 60:1 and of things which are often related to God: name (Ps 8:2, 10) Hs 9, 18, 5; D 14:3; light 1 Pt 2:9, cp. 1 Cl 36:2; glory Hm 12, 4, 2; course of action (the fem. for the neuter as a result of literal transl. fr. the Hebr.; s. B-D-F §138, 2; Rob. 254) Mt 21:42; Mk 12:11 (both Ps 117:23); the judgment day B 6:4; the deeds of God Rv 15:3 (cp. Tob 12:22 BA; Sir 11:4; Ael. Aristid. 48, 30 K.=24 p. 473 D.).—W. μέγας (as in some of the pass. already mentioned; cp. also SIG 1073, 26 μέγα τι καὶ θαυμαστόν; LXX; Philo, Mos. 2, 10; Just., D 10, 2; Tat. 25, 1) Rv 15:1; 1 Cl 26:1; 50:1; 53:3; 2 Cl 2:6; 5:5; Hv 1, 3, 3 cj. Joly. W. μέγας and ἰσχυρός Hm 12, 4, 2. W. μακάριος 1 Cl 35:1. W. παράδοξος (Menand., Fgm. 466 Kö.; Just., D. 133, 1) Dg 5:4.—τί θαυμαστὸν εἰ; what wonder is it, if? (Epict. 1, 25, 33; 2, 9, 9; 4 Macc 2:1; Philo, Aet. M. 137; w. acc. and inf. Ath. 30, 1) 1 Cl 43:1. ἐν τούτῳ τὸ θαυμαστόν ἐστιν the remarkable thing about it is this J 9:30.—2 Cor 11:14 v.l.—DELG s.v. θαῦμα. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hm Hm = Mandates

fem. fem. = feminine

transl. transl. = translate, translation

  1. fr. = from

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

  1. cj. = conjecture(d by)

Joly Joly = Hermas, Le Pasteur; s. under H—List 1

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

inf. inf. = infinitive

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 445
